blocks.statement: I can describe sound as a longitudinal mechanical wave, identify pitch with frequency and loudness with amplitude, and explain why sound needs a MEDIUM (no sound in space).

blocks.whatWeKnow: An asteroid impact generates HUGE atmospheric shockwaves and INFRASOUND (< 20 Hz). The infrasound from the 2013 Chelyabinsk impact traveled around the entire planet — twice.
blocks.connection: Today: the foundation. Day 17 applies sound + seismic-wave physics to predict the damage and detection range from an asteroid strike.

blocks.markdown:
## Sound, dissected

Sound is a **longitudinal mechanical wave**: a traveling pattern of **compressions** (molecules squeezed together) and **rarefactions** (molecules spread apart). A tuning fork pushes the air forward, pulls it back, and the pattern races outward at about 343 m/s — while each air molecule just wiggles in place.

The perception dictionary:

- **Pitch** ↔ frequency f. Higher f = higher pitch. Middle C ≈ 262 Hz; A4 = 440 Hz.
- **Loudness** ↔ amplitude A (intensity). Bigger A = louder, measured logarithmically in decibels.

And the boundary cases of human hearing: **infrasound** below 20 Hz, **ultrasound** above 20 kHz. Both are real sound waves — just outside our ears' range.

Because sound is mechanical, it needs molecules to compress. **No medium → no sound.** Space explosions are silent, no matter what the movies say.

terms.definition: A longitudinal mechanical wave — compressions and rarefactions in a medium (air, water, solid). Cannot travel through vacuum. A tuning fork pushes air molecules forward (compression), then pulls them back (rarefaction); the pattern travels outward at v ≈ 343 m/s in air.
terms.cognate: Sp. sonido · Pt. som · HC son

terms.definition: Our perception of a sound's frequency. Higher f = higher pitch. Units: Hz. Middle C ≈ 262 Hz. A4 = 440 Hz. Soprano high note ≈ 1500 Hz. Bass drum ≈ 50 Hz. Pitch is the PERCEPTUAL name; frequency is the physical quantity.
terms.cognate: Sp. tono · Pt. altura · HC ton

terms.definition: Our perception of a sound's amplitude (or intensity). Bigger A = louder. Often measured in decibels on a logarithmic scale. A whisper has small A; a rock concert has large A. Loudness is logarithmic in our ears: +10 dB feels about twice as loud, even though it's ×10 intensity.
terms.cognate: Sp. intensidad · Pt. intensidade · HC fòs

terms.definition: INFRASOUND is sound below 20 Hz (we can't hear it). ULTRASOUND is above 20 kHz (we can't hear it). Both are real sound waves; just outside our range. Asteroid impacts produce powerful infrasound. Bats use ultrasound for echolocation. Medical ultrasound imaging operates near 1 MHz. Infra = below; ultra = above.
terms.cognate: Sp. infrasonido / ultrasonido · Pt. infrassom / ultrassom

blocks.title: Sound is NOT made of moving air
blocks.markdown: Air molecules wiggle in place; only the COMPRESSION pattern moves. Your friend's voice doesn't blow air across the room at 343 m/s — that would be a hurricane. No medium = no sound.

blocks.instruction: Pattern shows alternating dense + sparse vertical lines. Label one COMPRESSION and one RAREFACTION. Mark the direction the SOUND travels — is it the same as the molecule motion, or perpendicular?
prompts: Draw bands of close-together lines (compression) and spread-out lines (rarefaction).
Arrow the molecule wiggle — PARALLEL to travel (longitudinal).

blocks.prompt: In a sci-fi movie, an explosion in space is shown with a loud BOOM. From a physics perspective, what's wrong?
blocks.given: Sound = compression wave needing a medium
blocks.work:
Space between objects is nearly vacuum.
Sound needs molecules to compress + rarefy.
No molecules → no compressions → no sound wave.
blocks.answer: The boom can't propagate. Realistic space explosions are SILENT.

blocks.prompt: A 1000 Hz tuning fork is held just above the surface of a lake. What are the WAVELENGTHS of the sound (a) in air (v = 343 m/s) and (b) in water (v = 1480 m/s)?
blocks.work:
(a) λ_air = 343 / 1000 = 0.343 m
(b) λ_water = 1480 / 1000 = 1.48 m
Same f, but λ in water is 4.3× larger.
blocks.answer: Air: 0.343 m. Water: 1.48 m. Same f, faster medium → longer λ.

blocks.prompt: PERIOD OF A TUNING FORK — A 512 Hz tuning fork is struck. What's the period of one cycle? (Use T = 1/f.)

blocks.title: Infrasound — the impact's long-distance signature
blocks.markdown: Below 20 Hz, sound waves travel thousands of km with little attenuation. NOAA's infrasound monitoring network has detected every major asteroid airburst since 2000.

blocks.prompt: Why is there no sound in outer space, even from a violent event? Answer in two sentences using the word 'medium'.
blocks.frame: Sound is a ___ wave (compressions + rarefactions). Pitch is the perception of ___. Loudness is the perception of ___. Without a medium, sound cannot ___.

blocks.title: Read & practice
blocks.markdown: Open the reader and read **26.1–26.5** (where sound originates, compressions and rarefactions, media that transmit sound, the speed of sound, and loudness in decibels). Answer the questions beside the reading as you go.
